rationale.R1.2: The causal argument that 'the structural marker — not the additional tokens — is responsible' is supported only by three prompt-ordering runs; checking whether that inference is logically warranted from the evidence presented is central to any reading of 'check this' on a research excerpt.
rationale.R1.3: Load-bearing: the 'state-of-the-art' and 'clearly outperforms prior approaches' claims rest entirely on comparisons where all three baselines are variants of the authors' own prior method (Anonymous 2023a); no external published baseline (e.g., Wei et al. 2022 CoT, Kojima et al. zero-shot CoT) appears. This is the single most consequential flaw in evidence-claim alignment.
rationale.R1.4: The paper asserts 'advances the state of the art in chain-style prompting,' but the comparison set is restricted to the authors' own FreeForm-CoT family. Assessing whether the novelty claim is defensible against the broader literature is a natural part of checking a paper excerpt making such a claim.
rationale.R1.7: All baselines are attributed to 'Anonymous, 2023a,' the authors' own prior work. No independent or external baselines are cited. This citation and scholarship gap directly enables the inflated superiority claims and is detectable from the text alone.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I check this for scientific validity (evidence quality, logical inference, novelty claims, and citation completeness), or for writing quality and polish (clarity, concision, sentence-level improvements)?
